problem: Should we restructure the repo's ~150 documents into centralized directories (docs/governance/, docs/technical/, docs/clinical/, docs/reports/, docs/specs/, docs/reference/) while keeping agent drafts in place? Husser would promote finalized docs during board sweeps. The goal is better discoverability without breaking code or the agent isolation rule.
context: Current state: 11 binding docs scattered across /docs/, /artifacts/, agent folders, and repo root. 45+ drafts in 7 agent folders. 25+ QA reports in Yuri's drafts. All agent outputs/ folders are empty — nothing ever graduates. Key concern from Brad: 'I don't want to hurt any code.' The proposed structure moves only .md/.sql documentation files, not code. But CLAUDE.md cross-references many agent folder paths that would need updating. The agent rule 'write ONLY in their own folder' needs amendment for the promotion workflow. This is a Next.js + FastAPI project shipping April 9 — Sprint 3 starts Apr 2.

agents.name: Husser
agents.framing: Owns document governance, Jira alignment, and sprint process. Cares about discoverability, stakeholder communication, and process clarity. Would be the one executing promotions during board sweeps.

agents.name: Luca
agents.framing: Owns architecture decisions, CLAUDE.md, and the agent isolation rules. Cares about not breaking the build, not disrupting Sprint 3, and keeping the repo maintainable. Skeptical of large refactors close to ship date.
